chapter_title.ar: باب اللِّبَاسِ الَّذِي تُكْرَهُ الصَّلَاةُ فِيهِ وَ مَا لَا تُكْرَهُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 60 - The clothes in which the Salāt is disliked, and what is not disliked

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/322)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/322)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) أَعْتَرِضُ السُّوقَ فَأَشْتَرِي خُفّاً لَا أَدْرِي أَ ذَكِيٌّ هُوَ أَمْ لَا قَالَ صَلِّ فِيهِ قُلْتُ فَالنَّعْلُ قَالَ مِثْلُ ذَلِكَ قُلْتُ إِنِّي أَضِيقُ مِنْ هَذَا قَالَ أَ تَرْغَبُ عَمَّا كَانَ أَبُو الْحَسَنِ ( عليه السلام ) يَفْعَلُهُ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from Sahl, from one of his companions, from Al Hassan Bin Al Jahm who said,

‘I said to Abu Al-Hassanasws, ‘I see the market display, so I buy socks, not knowing whether it is pure or not’. Heasws said: ‘You can pray Salāt in it’. I said, ‘So (what about) the slippers?’ Heasws said: ‘Similar to that’. I said, ‘I am constrained from this’. Heasws said: ‘Would you turn away from what Abu Al-Hassanasws used to do?’
en.sarwar: 31. Ali has narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from certain persons of his people from al-Hassan ibn al-Jahm who has said the following: “I once said to abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, that I visit the market and buy a slipper but I do not know if it is from properly slaughtered animal or not. He (the Imam) said, ‘Perform Salah (prayer) with it.’ I then asked, ‘Can I use such shoes?’ He (the Imam) said, ‘It is the same as slippers.’ I then said, ‘I am not comfortable with it.’ He (the Imam) asked, ‘Do you dislike what abu al-Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, would do?”’
